summary: All 5 workers completed successfully. High-confidence audit reveals critical gaps in premium model policy enforcement: (1) launcher default is banned Haiku, conflicts with docs; (2) metaswarm commanders may start on banned models if --model not set; (3) banned model enforcement is prompt-only, no runtime validation; (4) depth-guard-lint.sh does not verify actual model compliance; (5) user docs omit premium pool/banned list entirely; (6) child model rotation is non-deterministic; (7) no monitoring/alerting for banned model usage; (8) partial status only covers count, not model violations.
consensus_findings: CRITICAL: Launcher default model claude-haiku-4.5 (bin/stampede.sh:15) is on banned list for metaswarm children, creating immediate conflict
CRITICAL: No runtime enforcement of banned models - all validation is prompt/policy text only, violations trigger partial status after launch rather than prevention
HIGH: User-facing docs (README.md, AGENTS.md) never define premium pool or banned models, users must read internal agent specs
HIGH: depth-guard-lint.sh validates text presence but not actual model compliance or child-agents.jsonl entries
MEDIUM: Child model rotation at commander level is non-deterministic, could skip or repeat models without violating spec
MEDIUM: No monitoring, alerting, or recovery path for banned model violations

worker_atoms.key_finding: Model rotation is deterministic at launcher level but default conflicts with ban list
Ban enforcement is prompt-only, lint test does not validate runtime compliance
User docs omit premium pool and banned models, default model contradiction not explained
Commander-to-child propagation is policy-only, child rotation is non-deterministic
No validation, monitoring, or recovery for banned model violations
